summary: Parallel review: FAIL (4 critical, 11 warning, 8 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:288 — The uncommitted-revert test captures `checkout HEAD` commands but not `git clean -fd`; removing `simplify-pass.mjs:189–193` leaves all 46 tests green — extend test to assert `clean -fd` was called

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1578 — `phaseOrder` at lines 1578 and 1604 omits `"simplify"` despite `setUsageContext("simplify", null)` at line 1506; simplify-pass token costs silently excluded from per-phase console breakdown and progress log — add `"simplify"` to both arrays

**Architecture assessment:** The module boundary is clean, coupling strictly one-directional, guard condition correctly implemented and regression-locked, error containment two-layered (module + integration). The two 🟡 items — `git clean -fd` not asserted in tests, and `"simplify"` missing from `phaseOrder` — are genuine gaps that have been carried across multiple review passes without being fixed. Both belong in the backlog. No blockers to merge.

🔴 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:156` — `let revParseCount = 0;` declared but never read or used in the `"dispatches agent when code files are changed"` test; the `execFn` at lines 157–163 returns hardcoded values with no reference to this variable — remove the line

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:295` — `revertCmds` mock only captures `checkout HEAD` commands; `git clean -fd` (added at simplify-pass.mjs:189–193 to fix untracked-file revert) is never asserted and can be silently deleted without failing any test — extend capture to `clean -fd` and assert `revertCmds.some(c => c.includes("clean -fd"))`
- The 🔴 is a one-line fix: delete line 156.

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:295` — The uncommitted-revert test captures only `checkout HEAD` commands; `git clean -fd` at `simplify-pass.mjs:189` is untracked and unasserted — the fix can be silently deleted without failing any test. Extend `execFn` mock to capture `clean -fd` and add an assertion.

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1578` — `phaseOrder` still hardcodes `["brainstorm", "build", "review"]`; `"simplify"` absent from both line 1578 (console breakdown) and line 1604 (progress log). Simplify-pass token costs are invisible to operators. File to backlog. *(carried from multiple prior reviews)*

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:62` — File paths from `git diff --name-only` embedded in agent prompt without sanitization. Adversarial filenames can inject instructions when agent runs with `bypassPermissions`. File to backlog. *(carried from Security reviews)*
All three run_3 claims confirmed. Prior 🔴 (`callIndex`) and prior 🟡 (guard regex) fully resolved.

🟡 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:288 — "reverts uncommitted changes" test captures `checkout HEAD` commands but never asserts `git clean -fd` was called; deleting `simplify-pass.mjs:189–194` leaves all 46 tests green while silently re-introducing the untracked-file regression — extend `revertCmds` capture to include `clean -fd` and assert it

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:62` — File paths from `git diff --name-only` embedded in agent prompt without sanitization; strip control characters and newlines from each filename before embedding (e.g. `f.replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "").trim()`). Risk is amplified by `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` used at dispatch (run.mjs:290).

**Prompt injection (🟡):** `buildSimplifyBrief` embeds raw file paths from `git diff --name-only` directly into the agent brief. Git does not restrict filenames — they can contain newlines, null bytes, or markdown directives. Since `dispatchToAgent` runs with `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`, injected instructions in a crafted filename execute with full filesystem access. This is low-risk for solo repos, realistic for multi-contributor projects or submodule consumers. Prior security passes independently confirmed this finding twice; it is a known backlog item.
🔴 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:156 — `let revParseCount = 0;` declared but never read or incremented; dead variable (copy-paste artifact from adjacent tests that do use call counters); remove it

🟡 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:295 — `revertCmds` only captures `checkout HEAD` commands; the `git clean -fd` step (simplify-pass.mjs:189–193) is never asserted and can be silently deleted without any test failing; extend the capture or add `assert.ok(revertCmds.some(c => c.includes("clean -fd")))`
